title: Customize status options for end user portal
excerpt:
Description
The end user portal only has 2 statuses available. This should be customizable to reflect whatever status column we dictate in our board. For multi-step processes it would be nice for end users to know where …

posts.author: tommywu (Tommy Wu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

The end user portal only has 2 statuses available. This should be customizable to reflect whatever status column we dictate in our board. For multi-step processes it would be nice for end users to know where the ticket currently sits. Is it awaiting approval, being order, being provisioned? Just “Open” is not enough context.

What are you trying to achieve

Reliable live update of where the ticket currently is for end users.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
100% agree with this.

Right now the portal status is way too limited for anything beyond the simplest workflows. “Open” and “Closed” might work for basic ticketing, but the second you introduce a multi-step process, it completely breaks down.

If you’re running anything like:

  • Awaiting approval

  • In progress

  • Ordered

  • Provisioning

  • QA / validation

…end users are left in the dark. And that leads to the same thing every time:

“Hey, any update on my request?”

Which defeats the purpose of having a portal in the first place.

The bigger issue is that we already have this information in the status column on the board. Not being able to reflect that in the portal creates a disconnect between internal tracking and external visibility.

Ideally this would include:

  • Ability to map board status labels to portal statuses

  • Option to expose all statuses or a curated subset

  • Clear, user-friendly labels for non-technical users

End users don’t need full board access, but they do need meaningful updates. Right now, “Open” just isn’t enough context to build trust or reduce follow-ups.
